summary.hook: Google and NVIDIA both dropped flagship models today, yet gold's historic crash is drowning out the applause.

briefs.label: AI
briefs.text: Google launched Gemini 3 Deep Think, its most advanced reasoning model to date, on the same day NVIDIA's Nemotron 3 Super posted a 0.8 score on the GPQA benchmark.

briefs.label: World
briefs.text: U.S. bunker busters struck Iran's Isfahan uranium enrichment site as the conflict enters its fifth week with no ceasefire in sight.

briefs.label: Markets
briefs.text: Sentiment sits at Extreme Fear as gold approaches $4,554 per ounce yet records its worst monthly loss in modern history, whipsawed by war hedging and forced liquidations.

briefs.label: Wild Card
briefs.text: Amazon and OpenAI quietly unveiled a stateful AI runtime, a foundational shift that could let AI agents maintain persistent memory across sessions, arriving with almost zero public attention amid the geopolitical chaos.

focus_topics.title: Gemini 3 Deep Think Arrives
focus_topics.description: Google's newest reasoning model represents a direct challenge to OpenAI's o-series and positions Gemini as a top-tier chain-of-thought system. Launching alongside Lyria 3 music generation, Google is signaling a broad capability push across both analytical and creative AI. The timing, mid-conflict and mid-selloff, means enterprise adoption conversations will likely be delayed.

focus_topics.title: Isfahan Strike Escalates Conflict
focus_topics.description: The U.S. strike on Iran's Isfahan uranium facility marks the most significant escalation since hostilities began five weeks ago. Iran responded with a drone attack on an oil tanker near the Strait of Hormuz, raising the specter of sustained energy supply disruption. Trump's posting of unverified strike footage on Truth Social adds an unprecedented information-war dimension to an already volatile situation.

focus_topics.title: Stateful AI Runtime Implications
focus_topics.description: Amazon and OpenAI's joint stateful runtime project could redefine how AI agents operate by giving them persistent context across interactions, effectively a long-term working memory. This is a prerequisite for autonomous agents that manage complex, multi-step workflows over days or weeks. The partnership also signals deepening ties between the two companies, potentially reshaping competitive dynamics with Google and Microsoft.

ai_news.title: Google Launches Gemini 3 Deep Think Model
ai_news.body: Google released Gemini 3 Deep Think on March 26, a frontier model designed for scientific and engineering tasks such as spotting logical flaws in math papers and optimizing fabrication methods. The model is live in the Gemini app for Ultra subscribers, with early API access now available. It represents Google's push into specialized reasoning models alongside its broader Gemini family.

ai_news.title: OpenAI Expands Bug Bounties to AI Safety
ai_news.body: OpenAI announced a Safety Bug Bounty Program on March 26, extending its existing bounty framework to cover AI misuse and safety risks. The company also detailed its Model Spec framework, which outlines principles for model behavior, instruction-following, and conflict resolution. The move signals a more structured approach to external safety auditing as frontier models grow more capable.

ai_news.title: Amazon and OpenAI Build Stateful AI Runtime
ai_news.body: Amazon partnered with OpenAI on March 26 to co-create a Stateful Runtime Environment on Amazon Bedrock, focused on memory and tool-use infrastructure for AI applications. The environment is designed to support persistent agent state across sessions, a key requirement for complex agentic workflows. Availability is planned for the coming months.

ai_news.title: Google Ships Lyria 3 Music Generation Models
ai_news.body: Alongside Gemini 3 Deep Think, Google released Lyria 3 and Lyria 3 Pro on March 26, its latest music generation models. The dual release reflects Google's strategy of shipping specialized models across modalities rather than concentrating on a single general-purpose system. Details on pricing and availability beyond the Gemini ecosystem have not yet been disclosed.

ai_news.title: NVIDIA Nemotron 3 Super Hits 0.8 GPQA
ai_news.body: NVIDIA's Nemotron 3 Super, a 120-billion parameter model, posted a GPQA score of 0.8 following its release around March 11. The score places it among the strongest performers on the graduate-level reasoning benchmark this quarter. The model adds to NVIDIA's growing presence in the foundation model space beyond its dominant hardware business.

ai_news.title: Mistral Small 4 Posts 0.7 GPQA Score
ai_news.body: Mistral released Small 4 around March 17, achieving a GPQA score of 0.7 on the graduate-level reasoning benchmark. The model continues Mistral's strategy of shipping competitive smaller models that can run efficiently on constrained infrastructure. It arrived during a record-setting Q1 that saw 255 model releases across the industry.

ai_news.title: OpenAI Model Spec Details Behavior Principles
ai_news.body: OpenAI's newly published Model Spec framework lays out explicit principles for how its models should handle instruction-following, behavioral boundaries, and conflict resolution between user requests and safety constraints. Released alongside the Safety Bug Bounty Program on March 26, the document represents one of the most detailed public accounts of how a frontier lab governs model behavior. The framework could set a precedent for industry-wide transparency on alignment decisions.

ai_news.title: Late March AI Release Pipeline Goes Quiet
ai_news.body: No major new model releases were announced between March 27 and 31, following a burst of activity earlier in the month that included GPT-5.4, DeepSeek V4, and multiple Qwen3.5 variants. The pause comes after Q1 2026 hit a record 255 model releases. The lull may reflect labs shifting resources toward agentic frameworks and infrastructure rather than raw model launches.

world_news.title: Isfahan Uranium Site Hit by US Bunker Busters
world_news.body: The U.S. struck an ammunition depot in Isfahan, Iran, believed to house enriched uranium, using bunker buster munitions. President Trump posted footage on Truth Social showing 2,000-pound bombs detonating at the site. An American official confirmed the strikes to The Wall Street Journal, though no casualty figures or Iranian government response have been released.

world_news.title: Iranian Drone Strikes Oil Tanker Near Hormuz
world_news.body: An Iranian drone hit a massive oil tanker near the Strait of Hormuz on March 31, coinciding with the U.S. strikes on Isfahan. The attack on the tanker underscores Iran's ability to threaten the chokepoint through which roughly 20% of global oil transits daily. No details on crew casualties or the tanker's flag state were immediately available.

world_news.title: Gold Nears $4,554 but Posts Historic Monthly Loss
world_news.body: Gold traded up 1% to approximately $4,553.69 per ounce early Tuesday, yet the metal is on track for its worst monthly decline since 2008. Front-month futures rose 0.6% by 3:30 a.m. ET. The paradox of rising daily prices alongside a steep monthly drawdown reflects whiplash-level volatility driven by the U.S.-Iran war and shifting risk sentiment.

world_news.title: Trump Posts Unverified Strike Footage on Truth Social
world_news.body: President Trump shared video on Truth Social showing explosions from the Isfahan bunker buster strikes before any official Pentagon briefing. The post included no casualty data, damage assessment, or operational context. The move continues a pattern of the president using personal social media to disclose sensitive military operations in real time.

world_news.title: US-Iran War at Five Weeks With No Ceasefire
world_news.body: The conflict between the United States and Iran has now entered its fifth week with no diplomatic off-ramp in sight. Simultaneous escalation on both sides, U.S. strikes on nuclear-linked sites and Iranian drone attacks on commercial shipping, signals a widening war rather than a stabilizing one. Global energy markets, shipping insurance rates, and commodity prices remain in turmoil.

world_news.title: Hormuz Shipping Risk Intensifies After Tanker Attack
world_news.body: The drone strike on an oil tanker near the Strait of Hormuz raises the threat level for commercial vessels transiting the waterway. Shipping insurers have already hiked war-risk premiums in recent weeks, and a direct hit on a large tanker could accelerate rerouting around the Cape of Good Hope. Energy analysts warn that sustained disruption at Hormuz would push oil well above current levels and strain global supply chains further.

competitive.name: OpenAI
competitive.status: New safety bug bounty program targets AI misuse, not just code vulnerabilities
competitive.body: On March 26, OpenAI launched a public Safety Bug Bounty Program designed to surface AI misuse and safety risks, a notable expansion beyond the traditional scope of vulnerability disclosure. The program is paired with the release of OpenAI's "Model Spec," a document codifying the company's philosophy on how models should behave and where safety boundaries lie. Separately, OpenAI confirmed it is winding down the Sora video generator in favor of a new model codenamed "Spud," expected imminently, as the company consolidates around its $110 billion funding round backed by Amazon, Nvidia, and SoftBank.

competitive.name: Anthropic
competitive.status: Claude's legal win against Pentagon supply chain restrictions surfaces in court filings
competitive.body: Anthropic secured a preliminary injunction against the US Government and Pentagon over supply chain risk designations that threatened to limit Claude's deployment in federal contexts. While the ruling predates this week, the legal precedent is significant: it marks the first time a frontier AI lab has successfully challenged federal procurement restrictions in court. Combined with Claude's continued dominance in coding benchmarks and the rollout of features like Code Channels and Cowork Projects, Anthropic is building both technical and institutional leverage simultaneously.

tools.title: Zapier Central: Chain AI Agents Across 8,000+ Apps
tools.body: Zapier's AI orchestration layer now connects over 8,000 apps with natural language instructions, letting you build multi-step automations without code. Start by describing a workflow in plain English, such as 'when a lead fills out a Typeform, enrich it with Clearbit, score it, and route it to the right Slack channel.' The platform handles the agent logic, error handling, and retries automatically.

tools.title: Manus: Deploy End-to-End Research Agents Without Infrastructure
tools.body: Manus is an AI agent platform designed for autonomous task completion, from deep web research to structured report generation. Assign it a complex brief like competitive analysis or regulatory review, and it will plan sub-tasks, gather sources, and compile a deliverable. Best used for research-heavy workflows where you need cited, structured output rather than raw chat responses.

tools.title: Cursor: Accelerate Development With Inline AI Pair Programming
tools.body: Cursor integrates frontier-level code generation directly into your editor, offering tab-complete suggestions that understand your full codebase context. Use its chat panel to refactor entire files or generate tests by referencing specific functions. Developers report 30-50% faster iteration cycles when combining Cursor's autocomplete with its codebase-aware Q&A mode.

tools.title: Comet Browser: Run Agentic Tasks From Your Browser for Free
tools.body: Comet Browser offers a free agentic browsing mode that can autonomously navigate websites, extract data, and complete multi-step web tasks on your behalf. Point it at a comparison shopping task, a form-filling sequence, or a data scraping job and let it execute. It is particularly useful for repetitive browser-based workflows that do not justify building a full automation pipeline.

tools.title: Claude Artifacts: Generate and Iterate on Documents and Code in Real Time
tools.body: Claude's Artifacts feature renders live previews of code, documents, and visualizations directly in the chat window, turning conversations into working prototypes. Use it to draft an interactive SVG diagram, a React component, or a formatted report, then iterate with follow-up prompts. The key workflow tip: ask Claude to produce an Artifact first, then refine it in-context rather than starting from scratch each time.

tools.title: Notion AI as a Second Brain: Consolidate Knowledge Before Automating
tools.body: Before layering agentic tools on top of your workflow, centralize your notes, docs, and project data in Notion AI so its built-in assistant can surface relevant context automatically. The highest-leverage move is connecting meeting notes, project specs, and reference docs into linked databases, then querying Notion AI to synthesize status updates or draft briefs. Automation works best when it draws from a single organized source of truth.

newsletters.name: TLDR
headlines: Consumer sentiment falls 6%, hit by declining stocks and higher gas prices
Open internet advertising shifts from impressions to measurable impact
Marriott bets on real-time customer experience to industrialize intimacy
newsletters.deep_dives: Consumer sentiment dropped 6% to its lowest level of the year as the Iran war drove up gas prices and eroded stock values, with short-term economic outlooks falling 14% and personal finance expectations declining 10%. Middle- and upper-income households were hit hardest, and headline inflation is expected to reach 4.2% with growth slowing to 2% this year. Separately, open internet advertising is evolving as AI connects media and performance into a continuous optimization loop, making the open web more competitive with walled gardens like Google and Meta.
quick_hits: A B2B email nurture sequence framework recommends segmenting by problem, job, vertical, or company size, sending from a real human, and treating each email as an experiment with testable CTAs and formats.
Most CXO newsletters fail because they skip validation and publish generic content that drives no pipeline.
Asana launched AI Teammates for marketers, offering out-of-the-box agents for campaign brief writing, brand auditing, and competitive market research.
tools: Asana AI Teammates: AI agents for marketers handling campaign briefs, brand audits, and competitive research
newsletters.quote: Most CXO newsletters fail because they skip validation and publish generic content that drives no pipeline.

newsletters.name: TLDR
headlines: Apple pivots AI strategy to App Store and search-like platform approach
OpenAI kills Sora, its most hyped product since ChatGPT, to free up compute
Stealthy startup R3 pitched brainless human clones as organ backups
newsletters.deep_dives: Apple is leaning into its core hardware-and-services model rather than competing head-to-head on AI capabilities. The company plans to embed just enough AI in its operating systems to retain users while opening Siri and Apple Intelligence to third-party services, effectively turning AI into a platform play similar to the App Store and Search. Meanwhile, OpenAI abruptly shut down Sora, its video generation product, because it consumed too much compute and was not profitable, blindsiding even Disney executives who had signed on as partners.
quick_hits: OpenAI's Sora shutdown caught Disney executives off guard, with many learning of the decision less than an hour before the announcement.

A California startup called R3 raised funds to create non-sentient monkey 'organ sacks' as an alternative to animal testing, while secretly pitching a vision of brainless human clones as body backups.
Apple acknowledges its homegrown AI lags behind competitors and is focusing on ecosystem control and customization rather than building frontier models.
tools: Wispr Flow: voice-to-text tool that converts speech into clean, sendable text across all apps and devices, claiming 89% of messages are sent with zero edits

newsletters.name: The Rundown AI
headlines: Inside Sora's $1M-a-day collapse at OpenAI, with Disney blindsided by sudden shutdown
Microsoft pits Claude against ChatGPT for research capabilities
newsletters.deep_dives: A Wall Street Journal investigation revealed the chaotic behind-the-scenes story of OpenAI's Sora video generator shutdown. The product was burning roughly $1 million per day in compute costs, and Disney, which had an enterprise pilot in progress, learned the product was dead less than an hour before the public announcement. An internal code-named model reportedly required Sora's compute budget, forcing the sudden shift away from the once-viral video platform.
quick_hits: Microsoft is positioning Anthropic's Claude as a competitor to ChatGPT for research tasks.
Perplexity Computer can now be used to build travel itineraries, showcasing expanded agentic capabilities.
Stanford research highlights a systemic 'people-pleasing' problem in AI models, raising concerns about sycophantic behavior.
tools: Perplexity Computer: demonstrated building a travel itinerary via agentic workflow
You.com: featured sponsor promoting AI grounding resources
4 additional new AI tools mentioned but not detailed in the truncated content

newsletters.name: The Rundown AI
headlines: Physical Intelligence in talks to raise $1B at $11B+ valuation, nearly doubling its worth in four months
Snail-like robot designed to crawl through the gut to fight cancer
LimX's 'Luna' humanoid robot debuts on the catwalk
newsletters.deep_dives: Physical Intelligence, a two-year-old San Francisco lab founded by ex-Google DeepMind scientists, is negotiating a roughly $1B raise at a valuation exceeding $11B, up from $5.6B just four months ago. The startup is building AI foundation models to control fleets of general-purpose robots for tasks ranging from household chores to industrial workloads, with Peter Thiel's Founders Fund and Lightspeed Venture Partners reportedly participating. The deal intensifies competition with rivals Skild AI, Figure, and Tesla's Optimus program.
quick_hits: Violinists now have purpose-built exoskeletons to support performance and reduce strain
A snail-inspired robot is being developed to navigate the human gut for cancer treatment
LimX Dynamics showcased its 'Luna' humanoid robot walking a fashion catwalk

newsletters.name: TLDR
headlines: Anthropic's 'Claude Mythos' leaked as a new model tier larger and more intelligent than Opus, with dramatically higher scores on coding, academic reasoning, and cybersecurity benchmarks. The model is compute-intensive and expensive; Anthropic is working on efficiency before a general release.

Meta's Avocado 9B model pushed back to at least May as it still falls short of leading competitors. Meta is running parallel experiments with multiple Avocado variants and has reportedly discussed temporarily licensing Google's Gemini technology, with some Meta AI requests already routed through Gemini.

Anthropic's Claude paid subscriptions have more than doubled this year, with the majority of new subscribers joining the lowest tier. OpenAI remains the largest consumer AI platform but Claude's growth rate is accelerating.
newsletters.deep_dives: AutoBe, an open-source AI agent that generates complete backends from natural language, demonstrated a harness that boosted qwen3-coder-next's function calling success rate from 6.75% to over 99.8%. The approach uses type schemas to constrain outputs and compilers to verify results, showing how structured harnesses can dramatically improve unreliable model outputs for code generation tasks.
quick_hits: The last original xAI cofounder has exited the company.
Black Duck Signal launched as an agentic AppSec tool combining LLM-powered code analysis with 20+ years of human-vetted security intelligence to find and fix vulnerabilities in AI-generated code.
Lessons from OpenAI highlighted in the newsletter's subject line, though details were truncated in the content.
tools: Black Duck Signal: agentic application security tool for AI-native development that analyzes and fixes vulnerabilities in AI-generated code using natural language prompts in IDEs
AutoBe: open-source AI agent that generates complete backends from a single natural language conversation, with a function calling harness that dramatically improves model reliability

newsletters.name: TLDR
headlines: NYSE-parent Intercontinental Exchange completes $2B investment in Polymarket as prediction markets go mainstream
Revolut profit soars to record $2.3 billion in 2025, plans UK lending expansion
Mastercard bids to offload Nets payments unit originally bought for $3.2B
newsletters.deep_dives: Agentic payment infrastructure is emerging as a way to unlock new monetization models for content platforms beyond traditional subscriptions. The shift enables configurable, granular payment options such as pay-per-article, pay-to-preview for AI crawlers, paid comments, and tipping, allowing creators to monetize without sacrificing reach. This represents a move from fixed monetization toward flexible, programmable commerce layers.
quick_hits: ICE invested an additional $600M into Polymarket, completing a $2B commitment and signaling institutional adoption of prediction markets as a data and trading layer.
Revolut is rolling out credit cards, personal loans, and overdrafts in the UK as it pushes to convert millions of secondary users into full-service banking customers.
Mastercard is seeking a buyer for the real-time payments business it acquired from Danish company Nets in 2019 for $3.2 billion.
tools: Persona: KYC compliance platform offering a playbook for fintechs to streamline onboarding, assess AML risk, and build fraud prevention strategies across multiple jurisdictions
